notableFindings: Uploaded EV/card-counting hit-stay was a small improvement when fused with our threat targeting.
Hidden-state target rollout oracle showed large upside, but deployable belief rollout did not beat hand threat targeting.
Fresh win/loss-only league CEM learned nontrivial play but did not beat engineered production policy in multiplayer pools.

productionHard.policy: Bucketed solo-inspired board-efficiency / reveal-geometry / triplet policy; directional opponent component for 5+ players
productionHard.note: Directional features model next-player discard feeding and previous-player supply. They helped meaningfully in 5+ player games but not in 2–4p.

notableFindings: Column/triplet/end-risk V2 improved substantially over threshold bots.
The uploaded strategy policy was stronger than V2 in multiplayer and was temporarily promoted.
Solo board-efficiency and reveal-geometry training transferred extremely well to multiplayer.
Bucketed polish showed that 2–4p and 5+p prefer different tempo/risk settings.
Training the low-card-to-hidden behavior improved over the hand patch and fixed the observed strategic flaw.
Directional opponent modeling is useful in larger games; the 5+ bucket now includes it.
